description.en: A rectangular Languedoc loaf, split into quarters by folding rather than scoring, with an unusually large proportion of levain that gives it a mild sourness and a wide-open, glossy crumb. Keeps remarkably well without hardening.
description.de: Ein rechteckiges Brot aus dem Languedoc, das durch Falten statt Einschneiden in Viertel geteilt wird; ein ungewöhnlich hoher Sauerteiganteil sorgt für milde Säure und eine weitporige, glänzende Krume. Hält sich bemerkenswert gut, ohne hart zu werden.
description.es: Una hogaza rectangular del Languedoc, dividida en cuartos por pliegues en lugar de cortes, con una proporción de levain inusualmente alta que le da una acidez suave y una miga abierta y brillante. Se conserva notablemente bien sin endurecerse.
description.fr: Un pain rectangulaire du Languedoc, partagé en quartiers par pliage plutôt que par grignage, à la proportion de levain inhabituellement élevée qui lui donne un léger goût suret et une mie grande ouverte et brillante. Il se conserve remarquablement bien sans durcir.

origin.note: Traced to the 15th century in Beaucaire, once home to one of Europe's largest trade fairs on the banks of the Rhône.

ingredients.note: about a quarter of the dough's weight

keeping.note: Its high levain ratio was prized for letting it be stored without hardening, useful in the hot Languedoc summers.

summary.en: Have a generous levain ready, roughly a fifth of the final dough's flour.
Combine flour, water, levain and salt with a little yeast; knead 10-15 minutes.
Stretch the dough out and fold it over itself once, rest, then fold again from the width and seal the base.
For the classic look, roll the final fold through a little oil, bran or whole wheat flour.
Proof 90 minutes, then cut lengthwise following the fold line and bake hot with steam.

source.adaptation: Levain's own flour/water content estimated (assumed roughly 100%-hydration liquid levain) and folded into total baker's percentages.
